title: bee-hive routing/gates + go-mode + AGENTS managed block + workflow docs: Gate 4 session-only, merge guard, on-demand chain diagram
action: Re-encode the workflow contract so independent review is a user-invoked side entry, never a chain stage (SPEC R1/R3/R7/R8, 7.4, §11.5, per 565e68d0). bee-hive/SKILL.md: (a) Routing table — keep 'Review request -> bee-reviewing' (it IS the user invocation); add a row: a merge/ship/release request with unreviewed/stale candidates -> report count + risk level and ask ONE explicit question 'Create a review session for this scope?'; only an explicit yes dispatches; never silently spawn reviewers (7.4/A9); (b) Modes-and-Lanes ceremony table Review column — tiny: self-review + done-report (unchanged, it is verification); small: self-checks, no auto reviewer; standard/high-risk: review on user request (session panel per scope risk); Human stops become 1/2/3/3 + Gate 4 whenever a review session runs; (c) The Four Gates — Gate 4 belongs to a review session only (R8): it is asked when the user has invoked review, never after an unreviewed feature close; gate bypass never creates or auto-approves a review session; Gates 1-3 unchanged; (d) note in Session Scout: bee_status now reports review candidates + high-risk unreviewed warning (R7 — bee never self-dispatches on risk; it warns, states the merge/release consequence, and offers review). references/routing-and-contracts.md: skill-catalog row 7 'Load when' -> user requests review; chaining contract row for reviewing -> inputs: user-selected immutable scope (bee_reviews session), outputs: session findings/decision; recommended-next contract: after execution -> scribing (§11.5). references/go-mode.md: pipeline diagram STEP 6 becomes bee-scribing -> bee-compounding close (feature closes unreviewed with candidates reported); add a boxed note: independent review = separate user-invoked flow with Gate 4 inside it; go mode NEVER auto-enters review (R1) — go ends at compounding-complete + candidate report. templates/AGENTS.block.md AND the repo AGENTS.md managed BEE block — IMPORTANT: onboarding does NOT regenerate the source repo's own AGENTS.md (test_onboard_bee operates on tmp fixtures only), so you must HAND-MIRROR the identical edit inside the repo AGENTS.md BEE markers yourself; keep the two byte-consistent inside the markers and never write the arrow form '-> bee-reviewing' (or a unicode-arrow equivalent) anywhere — the side entry is written as 'on user request: bee-reviewing [GATE 4]': chain diagram '-> bee-executing -> bee-scribing -> bee-compounding' with a separate 'on user request: bee-reviewing [GATE 4]' side entry; Gate 4 line reworded to session context. docs/03-workflow.md: workflow diagram, bee-swarming handoff line, and the bee-reviewing section header/framing updated to on-demand semantics; status vocabulary verified/unreviewed/in review/reviewed/review stale mentioned once (R10). Verify AGENTS.md edit stays inside the BEE markers; run the onboard suite to prove projections still pass.

truths: no routing/chain artifact routes execution completion into bee-reviewing; the chain diagram shows execution -> scribing -> compounding with review as a user-invoked side entry
a merge/ship/release request with unreviewed changes is documented as report-status + one explicit question, never a silent reviewer dispatch (7.4/A9)
Gate 4 is documented as existing only inside a review session; bypass never creates or approves sessions (R8)
the AGENTS.block.md template and repo AGENTS.md managed block stay byte-consistent inside the BEE markers
go mode ends at compounding-complete with a candidate report and never auto-enters review (R1)

artifacts.substantive: routing merge-guard row, lanes review column on-demand, Gate 4 session-only wording, scout candidate note
chain diagram with on-request review side entry and session-scoped Gate 4 line
diagram + handoff + reviewing section reframed on-demand with the five R10 status labels

key_links: repo AGENTS.md managed block mirrors templates/AGENTS.block.md inside the markers
SPEC 7.4 and §9 supply the merge-guard and warning wording
prohibitions: Do not touch bee-reviewing, bee-swarming, bee-scribing, bee-compounding skills (cells 4-5)
Do not edit anything outside the BEE markers in AGENTS.md
Do not change Gates 1-3 wording or the bypass contract beyond the Gate 4 session scoping
Do not weaken or delete any existing test assertion

verify: node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_onboard_bee.mjs && ! grep -F -e "-> bee-reviewing" skills/bee-hive/templates/AGENTS.block.md && ! grep -F -e "-> bee-reviewing" AGENTS.md

trace.outcome: Re-encoded bee-hive routing/gates, go-mode pipeline, and repo/template AGENTS block so independent review is a user-invoked side entry (R1/R3/R7/R8), never a chain stage: routing table gained a merge/ship/release guard row (7.4/A9), Modes-and-Lanes Review column + Gate 4 wording moved to session-only, go-mode diagram's Step 6 now ends at scribing/compounding with a boxed on-demand-review note, and AGENTS.block.md + repo AGENTS.md were hand-mirrored byte-identically inside the BEE markers with the arrow chain retired in favor of an 'on user request: bee-reviewing [GATE 4]' side entry. docs/03-workflow.md diagram, bee-swarming handoff line, small-lane workflow row, and bee-reviewing section framing updated to the same on-demand semantics with the five R10 status labels mentioned once. Full verify green: test_lib.mjs 206/0, test_onboard_bee.mjs PASS (0 failures), both negative '-> bee-reviewing' greps clean.
